data.title: Star Equity Updates Investor Presentation, Highlights Growth Strategy
data.description:
End of Quarter Sale - 50% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

The latest announcement is out from Hudson Global ( (STRR) ).

Star Equity Holdings furnished an updated investor presentation on March 30, 2026, detailing its business and operations as of December 31, 2025, and clarifying that the materials are supplemental to its regulatory filings and not deemed filed under U.S. securities laws. The presentation highlights 2025 pro forma financials, including $224.7 million in revenue, $95 million in gross profit and $12.6 million in adjusted EBITDA, outlines a vision to grow adjusted EBITDA to about $40 million by 2030, emphasizes approximately $215 million of U.S. net operating loss carryforwards to enhance tax efficiency, and underscores a capital allocation strategy centered on organic growth, opportunistic acquisitions, and share repurchases, including roughly $10 million of stock buybacks since 2020 and an 8% block repurchase in the third quarter of 2025.

The company also notes the completion of its August 22, 2025 acquisition of Star Operating Companies, Inc., which now operates as a wholly owned subsidiary, and references a subsequent name and ticker change effective September 5, 2025, signaling a consolidation of its operating structure under the Star Equity brand. Management positions the group as a logical aggregator of select microcaps with an open-ended investment horizon, targeting outperformance of and eventual inclusion in the Russell 2000 Index, while acknowledging a broad range of operational, financial, macroeconomic, and regulatory risks that could affect execution of its strategy and financial targets.

The most recent analyst rating on (STRR) stock is a Hold with a $9.50 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Hudson Global stock, see the STRR Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on STRR Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, STRR is a Neutral.

STRR scores as middling primarily due to weak financial quality (ongoing losses and cash burn despite improved revenue and gross margin). Technicals are neutral-to-soft with the stock below key moving averages, while valuation is constrained by a negative P/E. The earnings call adds some support from improving adjusted metrics and buyback activity, but is tempered by a wider net loss and segment headwinds.

To see Spark’s full report on STRR stock, click here.

More about Hudson Global

Star Equity Holdings, Inc. is a diversified holding company that operates through four divisions covering business services, building solutions, energy services, real estate assets, and other investments. The company pursues a strategy similar to a private equity fund, focusing on scalable operating platforms, bolt-on acquisitions in select microcap verticals, and flexible deal structures using cash, debt, and preferred stock to drive long-term value.

Technical Sentiment Signal: Sell

data.title: Skillsoft Faces NYSE Noncompliance, Pursues Compliance Plan
data.description:
End of Quarter Sale - 50% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

The latest announcement is out from Skillsoft ( (SKIL) ).

On March 30, 2026, Skillsoft disclosed that it received a NYSE notice on March 26 stating the company is not in compliance with Section 802.01B listing standards, after its 30‑day average market capitalization and stockholders’ equity each fell below $50 million. Skillsoft has up to 18 months, subject to an approved business plan to be submitted within 45 days, to cure the deficiency, during which its shares will remain listed and trading, and the company emphasized that the noncompliance does not affect ongoing operations or SEC reporting obligations.

Management said it will evaluate all available alternatives to restore compliance, a process that will be monitored through quarterly NYSE reviews and could prompt strategic, capital or balance sheet actions. The notice underscores pressure on Skillsoft’s valuation and equity base but preserves near-term trading continuity for shareholders while the company seeks to regain its standing on the exchange.

The most recent analyst rating on (SKIL) stock is a Hold with a $4.50 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Skillsoft stock, see the SKIL Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on SKIL Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, SKIL is a Neutral.

The score is held down primarily by weak financial performance (declining revenue, ongoing losses, very high leverage, and deteriorating free cash flow). Technicals also reflect a strong downtrend, though oversold readings provide only limited support. The latest earnings call was mixed, with GK-driven weakness and cash burn partially offset by improving retention, AI platform execution, and modest FY26 free-cash-flow guidance (excluding GK).

To see Spark’s full report on SKIL stock, click here.

More about Skillsoft

Skillsoft (NYSE: SKIL) is a global provider of AI-native skills management solutions, offering unified learning, real-time skills intelligence and workforce insights to help enterprises build a “Skillforce” where humans and AI work together. Its personalized, interactive content spans leadership, technology and compliance training, serving thousands of organizations worldwide, including 60% of the Fortune 1000 and more than 105 million learners.

Newton Golf Company ( (NWTG) ) just unveiled an announcement.

On March 27, 2026, Newton Golf Company, Inc. removed Greg Campbell from his roles as Executive Chairman, Chief Executive Officer and principal executive officer, signaling a significant leadership change for the company. On the same date, co-founder and long-time Chief Technology Officer Akinobu Yorihiro, a former corporate M&A partner and media executive with extensive cross-border and entertainment industry experience, was appointed Interim Chief Executive Officer and principal executive officer, while Brett Hoge was named Chairperson of the Board of Directors.

The company emphasized that Yorihiro has no family ties or disclosable related-party transactions with existing executives or directors, and that there were no special arrangements determining his selection as interim CEO. His compensation remains unchanged despite assuming the interim chief executive role, underscoring a transition framed as a continuity-focused internal appointment rather than a broader restructuring of executive pay or governance terms.

data.title: Dolly Varden Silver Delisted After Completing All-Share Merger With Contango
data.description:
End of Quarter Sale - 50% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Dolly Varden Silver ( (TSE:DV) ) has shared an announcement.

On March 26, 2026, Dolly Varden Silver Corporation and Contango Silver & Gold Inc. completed their previously announced merger via a statutory plan of arrangement under British Columbia law, following all required shareholder and court approvals. Under the deal, all outstanding Dolly Varden shares were acquired by an indirect Contango subsidiary, with investors receiving 0.1652 Contango share or equivalent exchangeable share for each Dolly Varden share, and all Dolly Varden stock options converted into replacement Contango securities.

As part of the transaction, Contango issued 13,686,278 common shares, replacement options for 417,048 shares and its subsidiary issued 1,597,301 exchangeable shares to former Dolly Varden securityholders. Dolly Varden’s shares were delisted from the TSX Venture Exchange at the close of trading on March 27, 2026 and ceased trading on the NYSE American and Frankfurt exchanges, while Contango has moved to secure a Toronto Stock Exchange listing, consolidating its capital markets presence under a leadership team led by CEO Rick Van Nieuwenhuyse and President Shawn Khunkhun.

The combined company’s board now includes representatives from both sides, including Chairman Clynt Nauman and executives with deep mining industry expertise, signaling a governance structure designed to integrate Dolly Varden’s assets into Contango’s broader portfolio. For existing Dolly Varden shareholders, the merger marks a shift from holding a standalone silver explorer to owning equity in a larger precious metals vehicle, with future liquidity and valuation tied to Contango’s performance and its planned TSX listing.

The most recent analyst rating on (TSE:DV) stock is a Sell with a C$3.50 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Dolly Varden Silver stock, see the TSE:DV Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on DV Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, DV is a Neutral.

The score is mainly pressured by weak financial quality (no revenue, widening losses, and rising cash burn) and bearish technical signals (trading below major moving averages with weak momentum). A debt-free balance sheet provides some offset, but valuation signals are limited due to negative earnings and no dividend data.

To see Spark’s full report on DV stock, click here.

More about Dolly Varden Silver

Dolly Varden Silver Corporation is a Canadian precious metals company focused on silver exploration and development, historically listed on the TSX Venture Exchange, NYSE American and Frankfurt Stock Exchange. The company operated in the mining sector, with its shares providing investors exposure to silver-focused assets prior to its acquisition by Contango Silver & Gold Inc.

On March 30, 2026, Materialise reported the latest progress of its EUR 30 million share buyback program, originally announced on October 30, 2025, and executed via an independent financial intermediary. During the week of March 23–27, 2026, the company repurchased 50,994 shares on Nasdaq at an average price of EUR 4.26 per share, for a total outlay of EUR 217,478, with no cross or block trades.

data.title: Kirby Expands and Extends Revolving Credit Facility Commitment
data.description:
End of Quarter Sale - 50% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Kirby ( (KEX) ) has shared an update.

On March 26, 2026, Kirby Corporation amended and restated its main bank credit facility, extending its maturity to March 26, 2031 and increasing revolving commitments to $750 million while eliminating the prior term loan component. The agreement preserves existing financial covenants, including minimum interest coverage and a cap on debt to capitalization, and allows potential upsizing by up to an additional $500 million subject to lender approval.

At the effectiveness of the new facility, Kirby had about $200 million drawn, using the proceeds to refinance debt under the prior agreement, fund vessel equipment acquisitions, and support share repurchases, and subsequently repaid $20 million before the filing date. The revised structure provides Kirby with longer-dated, more flexible liquidity to support fleet investment and capital returns, potentially strengthening its balance sheet resilience and financial flexibility for future growth and shareholder-focused actions.

The most recent analyst rating on (KEX) stock is a Buy with a $147.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Kirby stock, see the KEX Stock Forecast page.

KEX scores well primarily on strengthened financial performance (improved profitability and sharply reduced leverage) and a constructive earnings outlook supported by strong free cash flow and capital returns. Technicals add support with a clear uptrend and positive momentum, while valuation is more neutral (P/E ~20 and no dividend yield provided) given cyclical and execution risks highlighted in guidance.

data.title: Sativus Tech Delays Annual 10-K Yearly Filing
data.description:
SATIVUS TECH CORP ( (SATT) ) has released a notification of late filing.

End of Quarter Sale - 50% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Sativus Tech Corp. has notified regulators that it will be late filing its Form 10-K (Yearly Report) for the financial year ended December 31, 2025, via a Form 12b-25 notice. The delayed filing relates specifically to the company’s Annual Report on Form 10-K (Yearly Report), not to a Form 10-Q (Quarter Report) or other periodic forms.

The company attributes the delay to events it describes as unforeseen, which have prevented completion of the annual report without incurring what it considers unreasonable effort and expense. No additional detail is provided on whether these are operational issues, accounting complexities, or external constraints such as third-party or auditor timing.

Sativus Tech Corp. states that it expects to file the Form 10-K (Yearly Report) within the automatic extension window allowed under SEC rules. Specifically, it plans to submit the annual report no later than the fifteenth calendar day following the original due date, which is the standard grace period triggered by a Form 12b-25 filing.

The company indicates that it does not anticipate any significant change in its results of operations compared with the prior fiscal year as will be reflected in the earnings statements included in the delayed report. Investors should note that this expectation is inherently forward-looking and remains subject to normal reporting adjustments once the Form 10-K (Yearly Report) is finalized and filed.

Sativus Tech Corp. confirms that all other required periodic reports over the past 12 months have been filed, signaling ongoing baseline compliance despite the current delay. The notification is signed on behalf of the company by Chief Executive Officer Michael Oster on March 30, 2026, underscoring management’s formal responsibility for the disclosure and its stated plan to complete the filing within the permitted extension.

More about SATIVUS TECH CORP

Average Trading Volume: 1,948

Technical Sentiment Signal: Sell

Current Market Cap: $379.4K

The latest update is out from Champions Oncology ( (CSBR) ).

On March 24, 2026, Champions Oncology, Inc., a global oncology-focused research services provider, reported that its Board accepted the resignation of director Dr. Philip Breitfeld and appointed Brian Alexander, MD, MPH, to fill the resulting vacancy. The company noted that Breitfeld’s departure did not stem from any disagreement over operations or policies, and that there are no special arrangements or related-party transactions tied to Alexander’s appointment.

Announced publicly on March 30, 2026, the move underscores Champions’ push to deepen its data-driven oncology strategy by linking its large portfolio of patient-derived tumor models with rich molecular and clinical datasets. Alexander’s background in oncology, data science, and AI-enabled drug development at organizations such as Valo Health, Flagship Pioneering, and Foundation Medicine is expected to strengthen the firm’s efforts to build a more connected translational research platform, potentially enhancing its value proposition for biopharma partners and reinforcing its position in data-centric oncology R&D.

The most recent analyst rating on (CSBR) stock is a Buy with a $6.50 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Champions Oncology stock, see the CSBR Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on CSBR Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, CSBR is a Neutral.

To see Spark’s full report on CSBR stock, click here.

More about Champions Oncology

Champions Oncology, Inc. is a global preclinical and clinical research services provider focused on oncology, offering end-to-end R&D solutions to biopharma organizations. The company operates one of the largest and most annotated banks of patient-derived xenograft and primary hematological malignancy models, leveraging proprietary in vivo, ex vivo, and bioanalytical platforms to generate high-quality data for oncology drug discovery and development.

Its strategy centers on an integrated platform built around patient-derived tumor models, enriched with comprehensive molecular and clinical datasets to enable advanced analytics and AI-driven insights. This data-informed approach is designed to support partners across the oncology R&D continuum, positioning Champions as a leading translational oncology research organization in a rapidly evolving, data-centric market.

data.description:

WPP ( (WPP) ) has shared an update.

On March 30, 2026, WPP plc filed a Form 6-K in the United States, formally incorporating a new base indenture for debt securities and related guarantees into its existing shelf registration. The documentation, involving WPP 2025 LLC, WPP Finance 2010 and several guarantor subsidiaries, sets the legal framework under which WPP can issue future series of guaranteed notes in the U.S. market, defining trustee arrangements, enforcement rights and covenants that will govern creditors’ claims.

The filing, backed by legal opinions under New York, English and Jersey law, strengthens WPP’s capital markets infrastructure and provides flexibility to structure and tap additional debt as needed. By putting a comprehensive, Trust Indenture Act-compliant platform in place with Wilmington Trust as trustee and Citibank as registrar and paying agent, WPP enhances its ability to access U.S. investors efficiently and standardize terms across future note issuances, which is significant for funding strategy and bondholder protections.

data.title: Arvana Delays 2025 Annual SEC Filing
data.description:
Arvana ( (AVNI) ) has released a notification of late filing.

End of Quarter Sale - 50% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Arvana, Inc. has filed a Form 12b-25 notifying investors of a delay in its Form 10-K (Yearly Report) for the fiscal year ended December 31, 2025. The company is late with its annual filing and also confirms that not all of its other required periodic reports over the past 12 months have been submitted.

The sole reason given for the delay is that filing the Form 10-K (Yearly Report) on time would require “unreasonable effort or expense” for a smaller reporting company. Arvana does not cite any specific internal control problems, auditor changes, or special committee reviews behind the timing issue.

The company states that it expects to file the delayed Form 10-K (Yearly Report) on or before April 15, 2026. This falls within the 15-day grace period allowed under the Form 12b-25 rules for late annual reports.

Arvana indicates that it anticipates a significant change in its results of operations compared with the prior year, as will be reflected in the earnings statements included in the upcoming Form 10-K (Yearly Report). Investors should treat this as forward-looking information that may differ from the final, audited numbers once the report is filed.

The notification notes that Arvana is working toward completing its outstanding SEC filings and bringing its reporting status up to date. The document is signed on behalf of the company by Chief Executive Officer James Kim, dated March 30, 2026, signaling executive responsibility for the disclosure and ongoing compliance efforts.

Oncotelic Therapeutics ( (OTLC) ) just unveiled an update.

On March 24, 2026, Oncotelic Therapeutics, Inc., a biotechnology company specializing in RNA-based cancer therapeutics and rare pediatric oncology, announced that its subsidiary Sapu Nano will showcase its Deciparticle nanomedicine platform at BIO-Europe Spring 2026. The company’s portfolio spans multiple oncology programs and includes AL-101, an intranasal apomorphine candidate for Parkinson’s disease and various sexual dysfunction disorders, alongside AI-enabled drug development efforts.

Sapu Nano was selected as a Presenting Company at BIO-Europe Spring and is set to formally introduce its Deciparticle platform and advancing clinical pipeline on March 25, 2026, in Theater A. The session will highlight lead candidates Sapu003, an intravenous everolimus in Phase 1 trials, and Sapu006, a Deciparticle-based formulation of docetaxel entering Phase 1, designed to improve drug distribution, safety, and efficacy, and Sapu Nano is using the event to seek strategic partnerships and co-development collaborations.

Sapu Nano, a subsidiary of Oncotelic, develops next-generation nanomedicine platforms to improve drug delivery and enhance the therapeutic index of established and novel drugs, primarily in oncology. data.title: Starbucks Shareholders Approve Governance Changes at 2026 Meeting
data.description:
End of Quarter Sale - 50% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

The latest update is out from Starbucks ( (SBUX) ).

On March 25, 2026, Starbucks Corporation held its 2026 Annual Meeting of Shareholders, where investors re-elected all eleven board nominees and backed, on an advisory basis, the compensation packages for the company’s named executive officers. Shareholders also ratified Deloitte & Touche LLP as Starbucks’ independent auditor for the fiscal year ending September 27, 2026, reinforcing continuity in governance and financial oversight.

In a notable governance shift, investors approved a shareholder proposal to replace supermajority voting thresholds with standard majority voting requirements, potentially making it easier to enact future corporate changes. However, shareholders rejected proposals seeking an independent board chair and several issue-focused reports on healthcare coverage, compensation gaps, diagnostic tools, and treatment of religious charities in gift-matching, signaling limited support for expanded social and policy disclosures at this time.

The most recent analyst rating on (SBUX) stock is a Sell with a $84.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Starbucks stock, see the SBUX Stock Forecast page.

Cass Information Systems ( (CASS) ) just unveiled an announcement.

On March 29, 2026, Cass Information Systems reshaped its banking leadership as longtime Cass Commercial Bank president Dwight D. Erdbruegger became executive vice president of strategy and business operations while preparing to retire in September, and Eric T. Giering was appointed his successor as president of the bank. In a March 30, 2026 announcement, the company emphasized Erdbruegger’s role in strengthening the bank’s market position and financial performance since 2018, and highlighted Giering’s three decades of commercial banking and risk management experience as a signal of continuity in the bank’s conservative credit culture, strategic growth focus, and support for client relationships.

The leadership transition positions Cass Commercial Bank to maintain its disciplined credit practices and tradition of stability while pursuing thoughtful expansion under Giering, who has led commercial banking at Cass since 2019 and previously held senior roles at several regional banks. For stakeholders, the move underscores the company’s intent to balance succession planning and strategic evolution with the preservation of its long-standing conservative risk posture and consistent value delivery to its enterprise clients.
